introduction.heading: School Introduction
introduction.lead: The CRIS Golf Program at Chiang Rai International School develops young golfers through structured, age-appropriate training across three program levels. Our coaching team combines international golf instruction standards with a nurturing school environment.
introduction.programOverview: Students train on-site and at partner facilities in Chiang Rai, with a curriculum that balances technical skill development, course management, and character building. The program is open to students in grades 4 through 12.

junior.label: Junior
junior.description: An introduction to golf for younger students. The Junior level focuses on fundamentals: grip, stance, swing basics, and etiquette. Sessions are short, fun, and designed to build a lasting love for the game in a supportive group setting.

intermediate.label: Intermediate
intermediate.description: Building on the Junior foundation, Intermediate students develop consistency across all clubs, begin on-course play, and learn basic course management. Competitive opportunities are introduced at this level for students who are ready.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.description: The Advanced level prepares serious golfers for inter-school competition and beyond. Students refine their full game — driving, iron play, short game, and mental approach — with individualised coaching and regular competitive play.

affiliations.heading: Affiliations & Accreditations
affiliations.lead: Chiang Rai International School is accredited by and affiliated with the following organisations.

wasc.name: Western Association of Schools and Colleges (ACS WASC)
wasc.description: Accreditation assuring that the school has clearly defined educational objectives and meets established quality standards through regular evaluation and institutional self-assessment.

asean.name: Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN)
asean.description: A regional partnership working towards peace, stability and prosperity, bonded together in dynamic development and a community of caring societies.

isat.name: International Schools Association of Thailand (ISAT)
isat.description: The unified voice for international education in Thailand, promoting high standards of educational quality and ethical practice.

opec.name: Office of the Private Education Commission (OPEC)
opec.description: Advances private education through system reform and quality improvement, supporting institutions across Thailand.

onesqa.name: Office for National Education Standards and Quality Assessment
onesqa.description: Develops national education standards and conducts mandatory external quality assessments every five years.

wida.name: WIDA International
wida.description: Supports academic language development and academic achievement for children and youth who are culturally and linguistically diverse.

handOff.title: Leaving the CRIS Golf Program App
handOff.body: You're leaving the CRIS Golf Program app and going to the CRIS website. Any information you enter there is collected by CRIS under their privacy policy — this app does not collect or store your data.

admissions.heading: Admissions
admissions.lead: Joining the CRIS Golf Program is straightforward. Follow the steps below to enquire, and our admissions team will guide you through the process.

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.description: The program is open to currently enrolled CRIS students in grades 4–12. New students may apply for school admission and golf program enrollment simultaneously.

steps.title: Choose a Program Level
steps.description: Review the Junior (Grades 4–5), Intermediate (Grades 6–8), and Advanced (Grades 9–12) program descriptions to identify the best fit. Our team is happy to advise if you are unsure.

steps.title: Contact Admissions
steps.description: Reach out to the CRIS admissions team using the button below. You will be directed to our enquiry form where you can share details about your child and ask any questions.

steps.title: Assessment & Placement
steps.description: New students may be invited for a brief golf assessment so our coaches can confirm the appropriate level placement. This is informal and designed to be a positive experience.

steps.title: Enrollment Confirmation
steps.description: Once placement is confirmed and fees are arranged, you will receive a welcome pack with program schedules, required equipment, and all the details you need to get started.

admissions.contactButtonNote: You will be taken to the CRIS enquiry form on the school's website.

tuition.heading: Tuition & Fees
tuition.lead: The CRIS Golf Program has its own fee structure, separate from the school's general tuition. Golf-specific fee figures are provided directly by the school.
tuition.annualNote: All figures are annual, per student, shown in the selected currency.
tuition.indicativeNote: CNY and THB amounts are indicative — converted from USD at a fixed reference rate and may vary with exchange rates. Contact admissions for exact figures.
tuition.exchangeRateNote: The amounts below may vary depending on the Thai baht exchange rate.

notes.golfProgram: Includes 5 rounds per week & cart fee

tuition.disclaimer: Golf program fees are separate from and in addition to standard CRIS school tuition. Equipment and tournament entry fees may apply separately.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.lead: Common questions about the CRIS Golf Program. Don't see your question? Contact us directly.

items.question: Is it possible to enroll even if my English skills are not strong?
items.answer: Yes. After enrollment, students will receive support through English courses to help develop basic communication skills.

items.question: Can I enroll even if my golf skills are not advanced?
items.answer: Yes. After taking a golf level test, students will receive personalized training from top tour professionals in the world.

items.question: What can you tell me about the student dormitories?
items.answer: The dormitory accommodates male students (2 students per room) and female students (2 students per room), with a capacity of 500 students each

items.question: What are the selection criteria for students?
items.answer: Our school selects students based on an interview and academic records.

items.question: When are the admission and transfer periods?
items.answer: The first semester begins in early August, and the second semester starts in early January. Rolling admissions and transfers are possible.

items.question: Can students go home on weekends or during holidays?
items.answer: Students at CRIS (Golf Program) are allowed to go home during extended holidays or special occasions, with prior approval from the Student Affairs Team.

items.question: How intense is the golf training schedule?
items.answer: The CRIS (Golf Program) golf training schedule is designed to match each student’s skill level. Students train daily, focusing on technical skills, strength, and mental resilience. Coaches tailor training intensity based on individual goals and progress.

items.question: What kind of support is available for students dealing with emotional stress or anxiety?
items.answer: CRIS (Golf Program) provides emotional and mental support programs, including Mental Golf Training & Counseling Sessions, helping students manage stress and maintain a healthy mindset for both academics and sports.

items.question: What special activities and recreational programs are available for students?
items.answer:
Beyond academics and golf training, students can join:
✅ Clubs & Sports Activities
✅ Art & Music Programs
✅ Recreational Events (Movie Nights, Cultural Exchanges, and Game Nights)

items.question: How is the safety of dormitories and the school ensured?
items.answer:
At CRIS (Golf Program), student safety is our top priority. We provide:
✅ 24/7 security with on-site staff at the dormitories
✅ Controlled entry system with strict access policies
✅ CCTV surveillance throughout the campus
✅ Faculty monitoring to ensure a safe and supportive environment

items.question: What are the meal and nutrition options for students?
items.answer:
CRIS (Golf Program)provides well-balanced, high-protein, and energy-boosting meals tailored for student-athletes. We offer:
✅ A variety of menu options, including vegetarian and allergy-friendly meals
✅ Nutritionally optimized meals to support optimal performance and recovery

items.question: How can students stay in touch with their families?
items.answer:
Students can stay in touch with their families via:
📱 Mobile phones
📹 Video calls
✉️ Email
CRIS (Golf Program) also provides:
✅ Designated contact hours
✅ Scheduled meetings with the Student Support Team for any concerns or updates

items.question: How does CRIS (Golf Program) provide healthcare and medical support for students?
items.answer:
CRIS (Golf Program) has an on-campus health center with medical staff and nurses available for student care. In case of illness or injury, students receive immediate medical attention.
We also have:
✅ A physiotherapist specializing in golf injuries
✅ Support for recovery and injury prevention

faq.moreQuestionsNote: Have a question not listed here?

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: The CRIS Golf Program app is a read-only information resource. It does not collect, store, or transmit any personal data. This page explains how the app handles information and where data is collected by third parties when you take action.

noDataCollection.heading: No personal data is collected by this app
noDataCollection.body: This app does not use analytics, advertising, crash-reporting, or any other third-party tracking SDK. It does not use cookies, session identifiers, or device fingerprinting. No information about you or your device is collected, stored, or transmitted by the app itself.

externalHandOff.heading: Admissions enquiry — handled by CRIS on CRIS's website
externalHandOff.body: If you choose to submit an admissions enquiry, you will be taken to the contact/enquiry form at golf.cris.ac.th/contact. That form — and any personal information you provide there (name, phone number, email address) — is collected and processed by Chiang Rai International School on their own domain under their own privacy policy. The CRIS Golf Program app does not see, store, or transmit that information. The hand-off is made visible each time: a confirmation dialog appears before any external link opens.

videos.heading: Gallery videos — YouTube Privacy-Enhanced mode, click-to-load
videos.body: Video content in the Gallery screen uses YouTube's privacy-enhanced player (youtube-nocookie.com). The player is loaded on demand only — no connection to YouTube's servers is made when a page loads. A connection is established only when you press the play button on a video tile. This limits, but does not eliminate, contact with Google/YouTube servers; if you require zero third-party contact, do not press play.

minors.heading: Children's privacy
minors.body: This app is an information resource about a school program that involves minors. The app itself collects no data from any user, including children. Photos and videos of students displayed in the app are published with parental media consent obtained by Chiang Rai International School.

contact.heading: Questions about this policy
contact.body: If you have questions about this privacy policy or about how CRIS handles personal data on their website, please contact Chiang Rai International School directly at admin@cris.ac.th or by calling +66 (0) 53 600-900.
